abstract: While deep learning has catalyzed transformative breakthroughs across computer vision, natural language processing, and information retrieval, these advancements have come at a significant cost. As models have grown in sophistication, so too have their parameter counts, inference latencies, and computational requirements. This evolution necessitates a paradigm shift: researchers must now prioritize a model's operational footprint as rigorously as its predictive quality. This paper formalizes the challenges of efficiency in deep learning and provides a comprehensive survey of five core domains—ranging from algorithmic modeling to infrastructure and hardware acceleration. In addition to reviewing seminal literature, we offer a practical, experiment-driven guide and accompanying codebase to assist practitioners in optimizing training and deployment workflows. To our knowledge, this is the first survey to synthesize the entire landscape of model efficiency across the full stack. By providing both a conceptual framework and actionable techniques, we aim to empower researchers and engineers to achieve immediate performance gains while fostering new avenues for experimentation in sustainable AI.
